title: Customers With Second-Highest Distinct Spending
description: Find all customers whose total spending equals the second-highest distinct total spending among all customers. If multiple customers share that second-highest distinct total, return all of them.
sql_solution: SELECT customer_id FROM (SELECT customer_id, SUM(amount) total FROM orders GROUP BY customer_id) WHERE total = (SELECT MAX(total) FROM (SELECT SUM(amount) total FROM orders GROUP BY customer_id) totals WHERE total < (SELECT MAX(total) FROM (SELECT SUM(amount) total FROM orders GROUP BY customer_id)));
pyspark_solution:
from pyspark.sql.functions import *
from pyspark.sql.window import Window

customer_totals = orders.groupBy("customer_id").agg(sum("amount").alias("total"))
max_total = customer_totals.agg(max("total").alias("max_total"))
second_total = (
    customer_totals.crossJoin(max_total)
    .filter(col("total") < col("max_total"))
    .agg(max("total").alias("second_total"))
)

result = (
    customer_totals.crossJoin(second_total)
    .filter(col("total") == col("second_total"))
    .select("customer_id")
)
